zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Preston於大約1338年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德普雷斯頓 帕特里克時，他的父親Sir John Preston43歲，母親Joan Capella43歲。他和Lady Preston至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1373年在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德維沙安享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: Preston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from one of the many places (most notably one in Lancashire) so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
occasionally from Middle English prest + son ‘son of the priest’. Priests were forbidden marriage but many produced illegitimate offspring.

variants.name: Presson
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): variant of   Preston  .
French: occupational name for a presser of wine or oil, from a derivative of presser ‘to press’.

variants.name: Prestwich
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place in Lancashire (now Greater Manchester), so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + wīc ‘outlying settlement’, or from other places with the same derivation. Variants such as Prestidge, Prestage, etc., parallel the local pronunciation of the Lancashire name. Compare Preston .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Preston was born about 1338, in Preston Patrick, Westmorland, England, his father, Sir John Preston, was 43 and his mother, Joan Capella, was 43. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Lady Preston. He died in 1373, in Heversham, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 36.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Prestonが約1338年にイギリスウエストモーランドプレストン プアトリックで生まれた時，父Sir John Prestonは43歳，母Joan Capellaは43歳であった。彼はLady Prestonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1373年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドヘバーシャムで36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Preston(이)가 대략 1338년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 프레스턴 패트릭에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Preston(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Capella(은)는 43세였다. 그는 Lady Preston(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1373년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 헤버샤에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.
